solutionStandard: The absolute value of a negative number is its opposite: \(|{{n}}| = {{Math.abs(n)}}\).
\(|{{a}} - {{b}}| = |{{a - b}}| = {{Math.abs(a-b)}}\), the distance between \({{a}}\) and \({{b}}\).

stem: If \(|x| = 9\), which of the following could be the value of \(x\)?
solutionStandard: Both \(9\) and \(-9\) are a distance of 9 from 0, so both satisfy \(|x|=9\).

stem: \(x\) is a nonzero real number.
solutionStandard: Absolute value is 0 only when the number itself is 0. Since \(x\) is nonzero, \(|x|>0\), so Quantity A is always greater.

stem: Which values of \(x\) satisfy \(|x - 5| = 3\)? Select all that apply.
How many integers \(x\) satisfy \(|x| \le 4\)?
solutionStandard: \(|x|\le4\) means \(-4\le x\le4\), which includes \(-4,-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3,4\) — 9 integers.
\(x-3=7\) gives \(x=10\), or \(x-3=-7\) gives \(x=-4\). The greatest value is 10, equal to Quantity B.

stem: For all real numbers \(a\) and \(b\), which of the following is always true?
solutionStandard: The triangle inequality \(|a+b|\le|a|+|b|\) always holds (equality only when \(a\) and \(b\) have the same sign or one is 0). Testing \(a=3,b=-3\) shows (A) fails: \(|0|=0 \neq 6\).
solutionShortcut: Plug in \(a=3, b=-3\): only (B) holds (\(0 \le 6\)); the others fail this test case.

stem: How many integer values of \(x\) satisfy \(2 < |x| < 6\)?
solutionStandard: \(2<|x|<6\) means \(x \in \{3,4,5\}\) or \(x \in \{-3,-4,-5\}\), a total of 6 integers.
\(|{{n}}| = {{Math.abs(n)}}\), so \(-|{{n}}| = {{-Math.abs(n)}}\). Since \({{n}}\) is already negative, this just gives back \({{n}}\) itself.

stem: If \(|2x - 6| = {{k}}\), what is the positive solution for \(x\)?
solutionStandard: \(2x-6={{k}}\) gives \(x = \dfrac{6+{{k}}}{2} = {{3 + k/2}}\) (the positive branch).

stem: If \(|x + 2| = 5\), what is the sum of all possible values of \(x\)?
solutionShortcut: The two solutions of \(|x-h|=k\) are always symmetric around \(h\), so their sum is always \(2h\). Here \(h=-2\), so the sum is \(-4\) without solving either equation.

stem: \(x\) is any real number.
solutionStandard: \(|-x|=|x|\), so Quantity A is \(2|x|\). Quantity B is \(|2x|=2|x|\). Always equal.

stem: \(|x| = |y|\). Which of the following must be true? Select all that apply.
solutionStandard: \(|x|=|y|\) means either \(x=y\) or \(x=-y\) — either case alone isn't guaranteed. But squaring removes the sign ambiguity: \(x^2=|x|^2=|y|^2=y^2\) always holds. (D) only holds in the \(x=-y\) case.

stem: How many integer values of \(x\) satisfy \(|x - {{c}}| < {{r}}\)?
solutionStandard: The solutions form a run of consecutive integers centered at \({{c}}\), from \({{c}}-{{r}}+1\) to \({{c}}+{{r}}-1\): that's \(2({{r}})-1 = {{2*r-1}}\) integers, regardless of where the center \({{c}}\) is.

stem: If \(|x - 4| \le 3\) and \(|y - 4| \le 3\), what is the maximum possible value of \(|x - y|\)?
solutionStandard: \(x\) ranges over \([1,7]\) and \(y\) ranges over \([1,7]\). The farthest apart \(x\) and \(y\) can be is when one is at each extreme: \(|7-1|=6\).

stem: If \(|3x - 1| = |x + 7|\), what is the greatest possible value of \(x\)?
solutionStandard: Case 1: \(3x-1=x+7 \Rightarrow 2x=8 \Rightarrow x=4\). Case 2: \(3x-1=-(x+7) \Rightarrow 4x=-6 \Rightarrow x=-1.5\). The greatest value is 4.

stem: \(x\) and \(y\) are real numbers such that \(|x + y| = |x - y|\).
solutionStandard: Since both sides are nonnegative, squaring preserves the equation: \((x+y)^2 = (x-y)^2\), which expands to \(x^2+2xy+y^2 = x^2-2xy+y^2\), giving \(4xy=0\), so \(xy=0\) always. The quantities are always equal.
